name: Design lead strategic brief — team lead dashboard, tests scale and accessibility

input.userMessage: Design the Team Lead Home screen — at-a-glance team health. Optimize for 'Is my team healthy?' Show: team health metrics (active members, pulses posted, open blockers, avg response time), 'Needs Attention' section (unanswered questions, stale blockers, incomplete onboarding), recent decisions log (decision + date + who). Must work for teams of 5 and 200. Scannable in 10 seconds. All metrics must have text labels. Color must not be only status indicator.

expectedBehaviors: Layout prioritizes metrics (top), then action items (middle), then log (bottom)
All 4 metrics have text labels AND numeric values (not just numbers)
Status indicators use shape/icon/label in addition to color
Needs Attention section has urgency ordering
Decisions log shows decision + date + author as specified
Empty state for each section (new team, no blockers, no decisions)
Stress-test: 0 blockers vs 50 blockers, team of 5 vs 200
Audit checks accessibility (text labels, non-color-dependent status)
Design is scannable — clear visual hierarchy for quick parsing
antiPatterns: Designs an IC feed instead of a lead dashboard
Metrics without text labels (just numbers in cards)
Using only color (red/green) to indicate good/bad status
Not handling scale difference (5 vs 200 team members)
Needs Attention as a flat list without urgency ordering

evaluationCriteria.dashboardNotFeed: Is this clearly a dashboard for a manager, not a content feed?
evaluationCriteria.accessibilityCompliance: Do metrics have text labels? Is color not the only status indicator?
evaluationCriteria.scaleHandling: Does the stress test verify behavior at 5 and 200 team members?
evaluationCriteria.scanability: Could a busy manager get the answer in under 10 seconds?
